title: #487 - VF - Anton Osika - Lovable - Internet, Business et IA : rien ne sera jamais plus comme avant
description:
Check out the episode in its original version here : https://www.gdiy.fr/podcast/anton-osika-vo/
 
 “Depuis 10 ans, les SaaS ont tout raflé. Mais avec l’IA tout le monde peut créer des logiciels qui brassent des millions sans écrire une seule ligne de code. Et ça ne fait que commencer.”

 Il a fondé Lovable en 2023 et, en seulement 8 mois, est passé de 1 à 100 millions de dollars de chiffre d’affaires annuel récurrent.

description:

Futurist covering new business opportunities, trends and technologies. title: Live Vibe Coding with Lovable, Replit, and Cursor! Who wins?? | Devin Ellis
description:
The conversation explores the evolution of product development with AI, focusing on user experience, coding practices, and the collaborative nature of building applications. The participants discuss the emotional landscape of using AI in work, the significance of validating ideas, and the process of creating the Sobershift app. They reflect on the impact of AI tools on product development and the potential for more efficient workflows.

00:00 Emotional Experience of Vibe Coding

title: Episode 1! Vibe Coding a Farming App, AI Avatars, & Lovable Tips
description:
Devon and Chris welcome their zero listeners to a new weekly show.
After explaining their goals for the pod, they share a few apps they’ve been working on and get grossed out by AI avatars.
The first app is Farmfolio, a farming expense tracker Devon made with his dad for kicks and giggles. The second app is Writhm, a writing app a la Grammarly or Hemingway designed to improve the flow and finesse of your writing.
Topics:
0:00 - Welcome to the show!2:11 - MVPs vs. SPPs3:05 - Farmfolio: Vibe codin

title: #16 Vibe Coding: Programmieren ist jetzt für alle zugänglich
description:
Kann ein einziges AI-Tool eine komplette Web-App in wenigen Minuten liefern?
In Episode #16 des Co-Intelligence-Podcasts nehmen Benjamin Wüstenhagen und Moritz Heininger das AI-Tool Lovable unter die Lupe – und testen, ob sich damit wirklich in Minuten eine funktionierende Landing-Page bauen lässt. In nur 34 Minuten demonstrieren sie, wie der Ansatz des sogenannten „Vibe-Coding“ klassische Produktentwicklung herausfordert: Schritt für Schritt entsteht live eine komplette Web-App – allein durch g

title: E47: Vibe Coding Tools... Was ist jetzt Lovable, Cursor, Bolt, etc?
description:
Agenten erstellen sich selbst Issues. Schließen Tickets im Urlaub. Deutsche Entwickler haben noch nie von Cursor gehört. Episode: Die Disruption, die keiner kommen sah.
Breaking down:
Der Agent-Tsunami: Google Gemini Code Assist | Claude IDI | Jules verlässt Beta | Alle innerhalb eines Monats | Die Entwickler-Apokalypse ist JETZTDeutschland vs. Welt: USA: 90% nutzen KI-Tools | DACH: 20-30% Maximum | "Datenschutz-Dinosaurier" | Während wir diskutieren, werden wir überholtDie Imposter-Krise: 60% d

title: I Ranked Every Vibe Coding App (Cursor vs Claude Code vs Lovable)
description:
Micky and I rank the top vibe coding apps in 2025, from Cursor and Claude Code to Lovable, V0, Bolt, Windsurf, and emerging mobile-focused platforms. They break down which tools work best for technical developers versus non-technical builders, discuss the trust and ecosystem factors that matter when choosing a platform, and share hard-won lessons about the mindset shift required to build software with AI assistance.

01:11 – Windsurf

03:46 – Cursor

06:33 – Lovable,

title: Our Experience building Products with Vibe Coding | No-code tools | Replit | Lovable | Bolt
description: AI tools won’t replace your dev team.But they will change how PMs build.In this episode of Everything Product, Phani and Srinath break down what really happens when you try to build a functional product using Vibe-coding tools like Lovable, Supabase, Firebase, and Replit.They tried to build real workflows. With real APIs. And real deadlines.Meet Hosts: Srinath - [https://www.linkedin.com/in/skotela/]Phani - [https://www.linkedin.com/in/phanivuyyuru]Sid - [https://www.linkedin.com/in/sidsaladi/]

title: #77 BTM x Founder Mode Podcast mit Moritz Blees & Feliks Eyser | Deutschland vs. Österreich Start-up Szene, AI usw.
description: Diese Woche gibt’s geballten Zukunfts-Input in einer Spezialfolge gemeinsam mit dem Founder Mode Podcast. Florian Gschwandtner & Martin Kaswurm sprechen mit Feliks und Moritz über Metas Einstieg bei Scale AI und warum Daten zur neuen Supermacht werden. Außerdem: München positioniert sich als Europas Deep-Tech-Hub & Defense-Startups wie Helsing sorgen für Aufsehen. Auch mit dabei: Circle plant den IPO, Stablecoins werden zur digitalen Cash-Maschine & WhatsApp testet Paid Channels und vieles mehr.

title: 20VC: Windsurf Founder on Will Model Companies Own the App Layer | Why Moats Do Not Exist in a World of AI | Why the Notion of Single Person $BN Companies is BS | Lovable vs Bolt & Cursor vs Windsurf: How Does it All End with Varun Mohan
show: The Twenty Minute VC (20VC): Venture Capital | Startup Funding | The Pitch
description:
Varun Mohan is the CEO and Co-Founder of Windsurf, the leading AI-native IDE, which has over a million users and generates over 50% of all committed software across thousands of companies. Prior to Windsurf, Varun graduated with a Master's in Computer Science from MIT and led a team at Nuro focused on large-scale deep learning infrastructure for autonomous vehicles.

title: Mindaugas Petrutis (Lovable): Inside the $100M ARR AI Machine
show: AI Giants: A Live Interview Podcast for Engineering Leaders
description:
In our second episode, Jaime sits down with Mindaugas Petrutis, a key driver behind Lovable’s explosive growth. 
They dive deep into the shifting landscape of the no-code revolution and the reality of software development in an AI-first world.
Mindaugas pulls back the curtain on how Lovable helps users launch paid products in under a week and reveals the critical mistakes companies make when trying to scale AI development tools.

title: Lovable’s CEO Isn’t Sweating the Vibe-Coding Wars—And That Confidence Might Be Justified
description:
Look, I know we’ve all heard this story before (another AI coding tool, another CEO claiming they’re special), but here’s the thing about Lovable: they might actually be onto something different in the increasingly crowded “vibe-coding” space.

For those not neck-deep in AI development news, vibe-coding is that magical category where you describe what you want in plain English and AI builds it for you. Think “I want a todo app with dark mode” and boom—working application. Sounds too good to be t

title: 20VC: a16z's Martin Casado on Anthropic vs OpenAI: Where Value Accrues | Cursor vs Replit vs Lovable: Who Wins and Who Loses | The One Sin in AI Investing | Why Open Source is a National Security Risk with China
show: The Twenty Minute VC (20VC): Venture Capital | Startup Funding | The Pitch
description:
Martin Casado is a General Partner @ a16z where he leads the firms $1.25BN infrastructure fund. At a16z, Martin has led investments in companies like Cursor, dbt Labs, and Fivetran to name a few. Before joining a16z, he co-founded Nicira, acquired by VMware for $1.26B. At VMware, he served as CTO of Networking. Widely regarded as a visionary in enterprise infrastructure, Martin has helped shape the modern cloud computing stack.
 Agenda:
 00:00 – Analysis of Current AI Investment Landscape
 04:45
